mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures directly increase corporate revenue expectations, which compresses valuation multiples and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with RSAFS growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 2.0 percent triggers immediate discount rate repricing across equity portfolios, forcing institutional deleveraging and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in total nonfarm payrolls and contraction in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that compresses corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k and UNRATE declines by more than 0.2 percentage points in the same month.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates deteriorating manufacturing capacity utilization, which reduces aggregate earnings growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in 60 percent of months where INDPRO falls below its 12-month trend, invalidating the supply-constraint earnings channel.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price index growth forces market participants to anticipate faster central bank policy normalization, which widens equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases consistently coincide with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns, yielding a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: New residential housing starts falling below 1.1 million units annually signals a sharp contraction in construction-related demand and household wealth effects, which depresses broad equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 65 percent of months where HOUST drops below the 1.1 million threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising core inflation and stagnant real GDP growth creates a stagflationary environment that forces equity risk premium expansion, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint CPIAUCSL-GDPC1 signal yields a positive predictive accuracy below 45 percent for negative 20-day returns across all historical stagflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent during an economic expansion triggers immediate recession fears, causing algorithmic selling and liquidity-driven drawdowns that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 records positive 5-day returns in over 70 percent of instances where UNRATE breaches 5.0 percent during confirmed expansion periods.
